remember: Students will be able to recall the key events and milestones in the contemporary history of South Asia.
Students will be able to recall the major political developments in South Asia since the end of the Cold War.
Students will be able to recall the key features of the major political systems in South Asia.
understand: Students will be able to understand the impact of globalisation and economic reforms on the economies of South Asian countries.
Students will be able to understand the role of non-state actors in shaping the politics of South Asia.
Students will be able to understand the significance of major regional organisations in South Asia, such as SAARC and BIMSTEC.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of democracy, authoritarianism, and hybrid regime to understand the political systems of South Asian countries.
Students will be able to apply the principles of good governance and human rights to evaluate the performance of South Asian governments.
Students will be able to apply the knowledge of South Asian politics to analyse contemporary issues and challenges in the region.
analyze: Students will be able to analyse the impact of historical events, such as partition and the Cold War, on the contemporary politics of South Asia.
Students will be able to analyse the role of major actors, such as India and Pakistan, in shaping the politics of South Asia.
Students will be able to analyse the challenges and opportunities facing South Asian countries in the context of globalisation and economic reforms.

ncert_objectives: To understand the major political developments in South Asia since the end of the Cold War.
To analyse the impact of globalisation and economic reforms on the economies of South Asian countries.
To evaluate the role of non-state actors in shaping the politics of South Asia.
board_exam_objectives: To describe the major features of the political systems in South Asian countries.
To explain the significance of major regional organisations in South Asia.
To discuss the impact of globalisation and economic reforms on the economies of South Asian countries.
